refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over agreed duration (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Require Site Manager and Safety Manager sign-off on compliance with worksite safety plan.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness sufficient for safe, productive remote task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall monitor and enforce worksite safety policies and alert operators to hazardous conditions in real-time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall collect and report productivity telemetry (task count, volume, time, safety events) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-performance autonomous/semi-autonomous vehicle with real-time teleoperation fallback, coupled with continuous safety monitoring and telemetry aggregation to achieve target productivity within safety constraints.
DP-B: Deploy a fully manual on-site operator model with enhanced vehicle instrumentation and safety interlocks, supported by periodic safety audits and manual productivity logging.
DP-C: Hybrid approach: on-site operator for routine tasks, tele-operator for high-risk or precision tasks, with unified safety services and automated telemetry collection across both modes.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate a high-performance autonomous/semi-autonomous vehicle with real-time teleoperation fallback, coupled with continuous safety monitoring and telemetry aggregation to achieve target productivity within safety constraints.

potential_sources: Vehicle autonomy level (DP) must be matched to operator skill and worksite complexity (FR context)—risk of over-automation or under-automation.
Teleoperation latency and bandwidth (DP) must support safe, responsive control—tight coupling with network infrastructure (IM).
Safety Services monitoring (DP) must not introduce false positives that halt productive work, nor false negatives that miss hazards—requires careful tuning and validation.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A is selected because it aligns with the NGHE mission (productivity, effectiveness, safety) and the high safety criticality tailoring factor. Autonomy + fallback + continuous monitoring provides both productivity upside and safety assurance. Coupling risks are manageable through iterative design and testing.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle autonomy algorithms can be developed and validated to operate safely in representative worksite conditions within project timeline and budget.
Assumption: Network infrastructure (5G, private LTE, or equivalent) is available or will be deployed to support real-time teleoperation with acceptable latency (<200 ms round-trip).
Assumption: Worksite safety policies are well-defined and can be encoded into Safety Services logic (e.g., geofencing, speed limits, proximity alerts).
Risk: Autonomy validation is time-consuming and may delay deployment if not started early.
Risk: Network availability/reliability in remote worksites may be unpredictable; fallback to manual control must be seamless.
Risk: Operator training for tele-operation and safety monitoring may be underestimated.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify autonomy level (SAE Level 2, 3, or 4) and define fallback behavior (e.g., safe stop vs. manual takeover) in the DP. Specify network SLA (availability, latency, bandwidth) required to support teleoperation. Define Safety Services scope (what policies are enforced, what are advisory).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system integration boundary that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules to deliver the productivity KPI. Owns the overall control logic, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits productivity telemetry (task count, volume moved, cycle time, safety events, operator mode) to Site Manager and Project Manager for KPI verification and operational monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task directives (dig location, load target, dump zone) from Site Manager or autonomous task planner, and routes them to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts from Safety Services module and broadcasts them to Operator, Tele-operator, and Safety Manager for situational awareness and incident response.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity (5G, LTE, or private network) to support teleoperation, telemetry uplink, and safety alert distribution.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level integration boundary. It owns the orchestration logic, telemetry aggregation, and KPI reporting. All sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within or interfaced through NGHE Solution. Minimal diffusion: only external stakeholder interfaces (Site Manager, Project Manager) and network infrastructure are outside.

Vehicle module owns the physical/autonomous control logic and task execution. However, productivity KPI reporting requires telemetry aggregation at NGHE Solution level, and safety enforcement requires coordination with Safety Services. Significant diffusion of KPI logic across Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

Teleoperation module owns operator interface and remote control. However, productivity measurement and safety monitoring are distributed across Teleoperation, Vehicle, and Safety Services. High diffusion of KPI logic.
Safety Services owns safety monitoring and alert logic. However, enforcement (e.g., halting vehicle motion) requires coordination with Vehicle module. Some diffusion of safety policy logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The FR (productivity KPI) is best owned by NGHE Solution because it requires orchestration across Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (operator control), and Safety Services (safety compliance). NGHE Solution aggregates telemetry from all sub-modules, enforces KPI measurement logic, and reports results to stakeholders. This avoids diffusion of KPI logic across multiple modules.
containment_risks: Risk: If Vehicle autonomy is highly decoupled from NGHE Solution orchestration, productivity measurement may lag or be incomplete. Mitigation: Define clear telemetry contracts between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

Risk: If Safety Services operates independently without coordination with NGHE Solution, safety alerts may not be properly escalated or logged for KPI verification. Mitigation: Ensure SafetyAlert interface is bidirectional and logged.

Risk: If Teleoperation operator interface is not tightly integrated with NGHE Solution telemetry, operator may not have real-time visibility into productivity progress. Mitigation: Push telemetry to Teleoperation UI in real-time.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the telemetry schema (task ID, volume, time, safety events, operator mode) that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services must emit to NGHE Solution. Define the KPI calculation logic (e.g., m³/h = total_volume / total_time) and the verification procedure (e.g., Site Manager sign-off). Clarify the orchestration protocol (e.g., how NGHE Solution routes Tasking to Vehicle and monitors progress).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (autonomous/semi-autonomous vehicle with teleoperation fallback and continuous safety monitoring) directly addresses the refined FR (operator-enabled task execution with measurable productivity and safety compliance). The DP is feasible, aligns with NGHE mission, and is appropriate for high safety criticality. No fundamental mismatch between problem (FR) and solution (DP).

diffusion_risks: Productivity KPI logic is distributed across Vehicle (task execution telemetry), Teleoperation (operator mode tracking), Safety Services (safety event logging), and NGHE Solution (aggregation and reporting). Mitigation: Define clear telemetry contracts and centralize KPI calculation in NGHE Solution.
Safety enforcement is split between Safety Services (monitoring/alerting) and Vehicle (motion control). Mitigation: Define clear safety command interface between Safety Services and Vehicle.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for this FR because it owns the orchestration and KPI reporting logic. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that contribute to the KPI but do not own it. The four known interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are sufficient to define the external contract of NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via representative operations trials. Productivity can be measured by logging task completion (volume, time) and comparing against productivity_target. Safety compliance can be verified by Site Manager and Safety Manager review of safety incidents and policy adherence. Telemetry interface (Telemetry out) provides the data source for verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The FR is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution, with sub-FRs allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The allocation is traceable and verifiable.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to specify autonomy level (SAE Level 2, 3, or 4) and fallback behavior.
Define the telemetry schema and KPI calculation logic in the NGHE Solution IM specification.
Define the network SLA (availability, latency, bandwidth) required to support teleoperation.
Define the safety policy encoding (geofencing, speed limits, proximity alerts) in the Safety Services IM specification.

decision.justification: The refined FR is not atomic; it has been decomposed into four sub-FRs (Vehicle task execution, Teleoperation control, Safety Services monitoring, and NGHE Solution telemetry/KPI reporting). Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to define the detailed design parameters and implementation modules. The DP and IM assignments are sound at this level, but the next level of decomposition is necessary to drive detailed design and reduce risk.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness sufficient for safe, productive remote task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall monitor and enforce worksite safety policies and alert operators to hazardous conditions in real-time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall collect and report productivity telemetry (task count, volume, time, safety events) for KPI verification.

assumptions: Vehicle autonomy algorithms can be developed and validated within project timeline and budget.
Network infrastructure (5G, LTE, or private network) is available or will be deployed to support real-time teleoperation with <200 ms latency.
Worksite safety policies are well-defined and can be encoded into Safety Services logic.
Operator training for tele-operation and safety monitoring is feasible and will be provided.
Productivity target is achievable with the selected DP (autonomous/semi-autonomous vehicle with teleoperation fallback).
open_risks: Autonomy validation timeline and cost may exceed project estimates.
Network availability/reliability in remote worksites may be unpredictable; fallback to manual control must be seamless and safe.
Operator acceptance of tele-operation and autonomous vehicle behavior may be lower than expected; training and change management are critical.
Safety Services logic may introduce false positives (halting productive work) or false negatives (missing hazards); tuning and validation are essential.
Telemetry aggregation and KPI calculation logic may be complex; data quality and timeliness are critical for verification.
evidence_needed: Autonomy algorithm design and validation plan (SAE Level definition, fallback behavior, test scenarios).
Network SLA specification and deployment plan (coverage, latency, bandwidth, redundancy).
Safety policy encoding specification (geofencing, speed limits, proximity alerts, enforcement logic).
Telemetry schema and KPI calculation specification (task ID, volume, time, safety events, operator mode, aggregation logic).
Operator training plan and acceptance criteria.
Representative operations trial plan and success criteria (productivity target, safety compliance, operator feedback).
